title: How did humanity end up underground in 'Silo'? Apple TV digs deep in new Season 3 teaser (video)
summary: Apple TV released a new teaser for Season 3 of 'Silo', exploring the origins of humanity's underground existence. The show's narrative focuses on uncovering the backstory behind the dystopian setting.

title: This X-ray image shows our solar system 'breathing'
summary: The eROSITA spacecraft has created the most precise map of soft X-rays over Earth, revealing a phenomenon driven by solar winds. This X-ray image illustrates our solar system's dynamic interaction with solar activity.

title: Viltrox AF 50mm f/1.4 Pro FE lens review
summary: The Viltrox AF 50mm f/1.4 Pro FE is a fast standard prime lens designed for astrophotography, offering competitive pricing.

title: How did the USSR win the space race to beat the US to the moon in Apple TV's new 'Star City'?
summary: Apple TV's new series 'Star City' explores how the USSR achieved early space milestones, including beating the US to the moon. The show highlights the ideological motivations behind Soviet space achievements.

title: Famous asteroid Ryugu may have been bombarded by a swarm of tiny space rocks 1,000 years ago
summary: The asteroid Ryugu may have been bombarded by numerous tiny space rocks around 1,000 years ago. Evidence from samples returned by Japan's Hayabusa2 spacecraft supports this theory.

title: 'Guess I'll be waiting a minute to surf again': Artemis 2 moon astronaut Christina Koch shares her recovery journey
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch shared her recovery journey after the Artemis 2 moon mission, noting difficulties in walking straight during a blindfold test as part of readjusting to Earth's gravity.

title: A Lego 'Project Hail Mary' set just soared into the stratosphere, breaking the Guinness World Record for highest flight
summary: A Lego 'Project Hail Mary' spaceship set a Guinness World Record for the highest flight in the stratosphere. The model reached an unprecedented altitude during its journey.

title: Ice melts in the springtime on Mars | Space photo of the day for April 24, 2026
summary: Springtime has arrived at Mars' Chasma Boreale, as captured in a new image by NASA's Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter. The photo highlights seasonal ice melt on the Red Planet.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship program at Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year as the first Block 3 flight approaches.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of the spacesuit development.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled across four countries and five launch vehicles during the week of April 20. The busy week highlights global space industry activity.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's plans to expand its space-based cellular broadband network.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission, marking the first reuse of its booster. The launch occurred just five months after its second mission.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an Astronaut Flight Suit and a Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality for future space missions. The innovations are part of their efforts to develop a commercial space station.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures on the International Space Station in March. The month was marked by significant activity aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn is preparing for its third launch with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding its CRS NG-23 resupply flight.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste achieved its first navigation signal, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement was strategically chosen.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved systems for navigation or communication. It highlights a breakthrough or innovation in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using ultralight graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel graphene aerogels with greater velocity and thrust in space-like conditions compared to Earth.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to reduce costs and scale operations. This strategy targets commercial and government customers, including potential military applications.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Donald Trump has appointed space industry executives to key military space roles, pending Senate confirmation. The nominees will require approval before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center, potentially powering future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center versus a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs. The study is presented in a Payload Pro article.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has raised €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have pledged to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, citing concerns that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet presidential directives. This marks another instance of bipartisan opposition to funding reductions for NASA.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships and suggests that the Navy could assist in tracking these satellites. It highlights the mutual benefits of cooperation between the Navy and satellite tracking efforts.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has raised €25.7 million in a Series A funding round, enabling the company to transition to regular operational service. The funds will support the development of ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3, aiming to build a commercial, institutional, and defense-capable space architecture.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, announced in January 2025, is a missile defense program aiming to protect the US, attracting significant interest from companies. The program is positioned as a major contender in the space industry for defense applications.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under the CLPS program, creating opportunities for various space industry companies beyond just lunar-focused firms.

title: GomSpace to Establish New Ukrainian Joint Venture for Sovereign Comms
summary: GomSpace, a Danish satellite company, is establishing a new joint venture in Ukraine to develop sovereign satellite communication capabilities. The partnership is with Ukrainian firm Stetman.

title: Rocket Lab Introduces Next-Generation Star Tracker Component
summary: Rocket Lab has launched its next-generation High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP) component, designed for extended spacecraft missions. This expansion strengthens the company's offerings in space navigation technology.

title: FCC Rejects Requests for Sharing in MSS Spectrum, Maintaining Exclusive Rights
summary: The FCC rejected SpaceX and other operators' requests to share MSS spectrum bands, maintaining exclusive licensing rights in an order issued Thursday.

title: SDA’s Need for Speed Pushes Startups for Results
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is pausing launches until May to address technology hurdles, supply chain challenges, and balance speed. Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo emphasized the need for startups to deliver results.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy teleport sites at Equinx's data centers for its Meridian constellation. This partnership aims to support the ground infrastructure for SpinLaunch's satellite network.

title: Iridium Continues IoT Subscriber Growth in Q1, Desch Talks NTN Direct Launch and Spectrum
summary: Iridium reported a 2% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 2026, driven by continued growth in IoT subscribers. CEO Matt Desch discussed plans for direct NTN (Non-Terrestrial Network) launches and spectrum discussions.

title: Univity Raises Further Funding for VLEO Constellation for Europe
summary: French startup Univity secured a €27 million ($32 million) Series A funding round to develop two demonstration satellites for a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) connectivity constellation in Europe.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: The European Union is drafting the EU Space Act, which has raised concerns among U.S. officials who view it as an overreach. The proposed legislation aims to create a unified regulatory framework for space activities within the EU.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Jetstar Airways, an Australian low-cost airline, has chosen Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed across 11 Jetstar aircraft.

title: SSC Space and Kuva Space to Collaborate in Nordic Region
summary: SSC Space and Kuva Space have announced a regional collaboration to enhance space-based capabilities across the Nordic region's space value chain. The partnership aims to strengthen various aspects of space operations and technology.
